name: Zayd ibn Yazid
title: Devoted Son of Yazid
shortDescription: The son of Yazid ibn Thubayt al-Abdi who, along with his father and brother, rushed from Basra to help the Imam and was martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Zayd ibn Yazid ibn Thubayt al-Abdi was a brave and faithful young man from Basra. He was raised in a house overflowing with love for the Ahl al-Bayt.

When his father (Yazid ibn Thubayt) decided to leave Basra—which was under strict control by Ibn Ziyad—to assist Imam Hussain (AS), Zayd and his brother Ubaydullah also volunteered to accompany him.

They cleverly bypassed Basra's security rings and joined the Imam's caravan on the way to Mecca. On Ashura, Zayd stood manfully against the Kufan army.

After a valiant fight, he attained the exalted rank of martyrdom shoulder-to-shoulder with his father and brother, and his name is mentioned alongside his brother in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى زَيْدِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ ثُبَيْتٍ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Zayd ibn Yazid ibn Thubayt...
